checks.a_analysis: The output faithfully follows the text provided in the source PDF and its OCR. I found no evidence of invented headings, body text, or other fabricated content beyond expected AI-generated alt text for images.

The URL 'https://myncid.nc.gov/' appears repeatedly throughout the source document as a login step. The output correctly transcribes this URL in every instance and does not invent any additional URLs, phone numbers, or email addresses.

The output contains 6 main sections corresponding to the 6 pages of the source document. All primary topics (Enrollment methods, Modification, Changing defaults, Deletion, and Application enrollment) and their subsections are present.
The source document contains no tables; instructions are presented in columns or lists.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: Crucial procedural images/labels for buttons are omitted from several subsections in the output. On Page 1, the 'Phone Call' subsection is entirely missing the four button images (ADD ENROLLMENT, ADD, NEXT, SUBMIT) required for steps 3-6. The 'Text Message' subsection is missing two buttons (NEXT, SUBMIT) for steps 5-6. On Page 3, the 'Phone number' subsection is missing the Edit icon and the 'SEND OTP' button. These represent significant omissions of instructional content, as the text instructions are left incomplete without these identifiers.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 1, Text Message, steps 5 & 6; Page 1, Phone Call, steps 3-6; Page 3, Phone number, steps 2 & 3.
checks.b_evidence_output: The 'elements' array for the corresponding subsections in the JSON is missing these image assets and their labels.
checks.b_evidence_location: Section 1 (Subsections 'Text Message' and 'Phone Call'), Section 3 (Subsection 'Phone number')

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: Instructional steps throughout the document are truncated or grammatically incomplete. Where the source PDF uses a button or icon image within a step, the JSON text string often ends abruptly or lacks the object (e.g., 'click on: ', 'click on next to'). While some of these images are included as separate elements, the textual representation is degraded compared to the source.

checks.a_analysis: The output correctly deduplicates common headers and footers (like the document title and 'How to enroll for MFA:' heading) that appear on multiple pages in the source. No significant text or element duplication was found.

The heading hierarchy is incorrect for the 'Authenticator' method. In the source (Page 2), 'Authenticator' is an enrollment method under the heading 'How to enroll for MFA:', which makes it a peer to 'Email', 'Text Message', and 'Phone Call'. However, while those three are correctly subsections of Section 1, 'Authenticator' is promoted to a top-level Section title (Section 2), making it a peer to the main document title section. This is a significant nesting error.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 2: Authenticator (method under 'How to enroll for MFA:')

checks.a_analysis: There are no tables in the source document.

The reading order for instruction lists is significantly corrupted in Sections 1 and 2. Images of buttons (e.g., 'ADD ENROLLMENT', 'ADD', 'NEXT', 'SUBMIT') which are integral parts of specific steps in the lists are extracted and placed after the entire list of text. This breaks the logical flow and meaning of the instructions (e.g., Step 3 ending with 'Click on: ' with the button following the whole list). Additionally, several button images from the 'Phone Call' subsection and edit icons from page 3 are missing from the structure entirely.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 1: 3. Click on: [ADD ENROLLMENT] (Image follows text immediately)
checks.b_evidence_output: Section 1, Subsection Email: Item 3 ends with 'Click on: '. The image is Element 1 in the subsection, following Element 0 (the entire list).

checks.a_analysis: The lists are correctly identified as ordered lists, but the conversion of individual items is incorrect. Steps that include images (buttons) have those images removed from the item structure and placed at the end of the list, resulting in incomplete list items that do not preserve the full meaning of the instruction step.
checks.b_evidence_output: List item 4: '4. Select “Email” and click:' (Missing image within item)

checks.a_analysis: URLs such as 'https://myncid.nc.gov/' are preserved as plain text within the paragraphs/items. No incorrect or fabricated links were added.

checks.a_analysis: The visual flow of instructions is significantly disrupted in the converted HTML. In the source PDF, functional buttons and icons are positioned immediately following the text for each numbered step, providing clear visual context. In the HTML output, these elements are either completely missing (as in the 'Phone Call' section) or decoupled from their respective steps and stacked at the bottom of the section blocks. This significantly alters the visual organization of information and the instructional flow.
checks.b_evidence_source: PDF Page 1, 'Text Message' section: Steps 3, 4, 5, and 6 each have a button image (ADD ENROLLMENT, ADD, NEXT, SUBMIT) positioned directly after the instructional text.
checks.b_evidence_output: HTML Screenshot, 'Text Message' section: Steps 3, 4, 5, and 6 end with a colon and no button. Two buttons (NEXT, SUBMIT) are placed at the very bottom of the section, losing the step-by-step association.
checks.b_evidence_location: How to enroll for MFA: Text Message section, Page 1

checks.a_analysis: The document contains numbered lists and instructional blocks but does not contain formal data tables. Therefore, there are no tables to evaluate for misrendering.

Numerous meaningful images and icons present in the source PDF are missing from the HTML output. This includes UI icons used as instructions (pencil, trash can, plus sign), functional buttons in the 'Phone Call' section, and descriptive screenshots illustrating steps (QR code scanning screen and success popup). These are not decorative; they are essential for understanding the instructions.
checks.b_evidence_source: PDF Page 3, step 2: 'click on [pencil icon] next to your email.' PDF Page 5, step 3: 'Click on [trash icon] next to the method...'. PDF Page 2, step 8: Shows image of phone scanning QR code.
checks.b_evidence_output: HTML Screenshot: The icons are missing from the sentences in sections 'Modify MFA...' and 'Delete MFA method'. The QR code scanning image and 'Success' popup image are missing from the 'Authenticator' section.

checks.a_analysis: There is no evidence of body content (paragraphs, specific instructions, or tables) being duplicated. While the MyNCID logo and main title repeat, this corresponds to the header appearing on every page of the source PDF and is an expected organizational structure for the conversion.
